cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the story carefully and try to find the answer. The author is talking about their parents who had a kidney transplant surgery. The mom donated her kidney to the dad. After the surgery, they were both in the hospital and the family visited. The parents were in pain, but they were focused on each other and showed a lot of emotion. The author mentions that their parents finally understood how much they loved each other after many years of quarrels. The author also says that the transplant healed the whole family, making the dad gentler and more patient. The parents have a closer relationship now and their faith is deeper. The question asks what conclusion we can draw from the story. The options are A) Illness is part of daily life, B) Quarrels can't be avoided between parents, C) The relationship between parents affects children greatly, D) Suffering can better people's relationship as well. Looking at the options, let's analyze. The story doesn't really focus on illness being part of daily life, so A is probably not right. B says quarrels can't be avoided, but the story mentions that after many years of quarrels, the parents now have a better relationship. So it's not that quarrels can't be avoided, but that they were resolved. C talks about the effect on children, but the main point of the story is about the parents' relationship improving.
